creator: brian@hyperreal.org
text:
When conducting a /usr/ports based install of ssh, a "make install" 
obliterates any preexisting host keys.  A "make install" should instead
look for any pre-existing keys and use them, or at least prompt to ask
if the user wishes to make new keys.

It could be argued that this is a bug in ssh, or that I shouldn't 
expect a "make install" to be nice about things like this, but I would
counter that it defeats the purpose of a "ports" collection as an
ease-of-use feature if I need to inspect closely every package I build
with it.

I feel guilty about complaining about an otherwise excellent setup.
